summary: Matched several CaptureDamage-related callbacks and helpers in ftCo_Attack100, mainly covering high/low capture-damage transitions, physics/collision callbacks, and Yoshi-specific captured hurtbox handling. The PR also exposed needed prototypes, added the capturedamage motion-variable layout, and annotated nearby Capture motion-state enum values.
what_changed: Implemented previously stubbed CaptureDamage and capture-position helpers in ftCo_Attack100.c, including fn_800DAA40, fn_800DAD18, fn_800DB5D8, ftCo_800DC284, ftCo_CaptureDamageHi_Phys, ftCo_CaptureDamageHi_Coll, fn_800DC384, ftCo_800DC3A4, fn_800DC404, ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys, ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll, and fn_800DC624.

Added Yoshi-specific handling in fn_800DB5D8: when the victim fighter is FTKIND_YOSHI, the captured fighter becomes invisible/intangible, gets accessory callback ftCo_800DB464, initializes a mid-height non-grabbable hurtbox on FtPart_XRotN, and scales that hurtbox using ftYs_Init_8012BAC0(victim_fp) divided by ftCommon_GetModelScale(fp).
Added capture damage state transitions between ftCo_MS_CaptureDamageHi and ftCo_MS_CaptureDamageLw, preserving current animation frame with Ft_MF_UpdateCmd where needed.
Added collision callbacks that guard on fp->x2226_b2 before calling ft_80083C00 or ft_8008403C with transition callbacks such as fn_800DC384 and fn_800DC624.
Added position synchronization logic using fn_800DAD18, fn_800DAA40, lb_8000B1CC, HSD_JObjSetTranslate, current fighter position, victim_gobj, and capturedamage.x18.
Added the ftCommon_MotionVars.capturedamage struct in types.h with fields x0, x4, pad_x8, and HSD_JObj* x18 at fp+2358.
Added prototypes for newly matched helpers/callbacks in ftCo_Attack100.h, including fn_800DAA40, fn_800DAC78, fn_800DC384, fn_800DC404, and fn_800DC624.
Annotated ftCommon_MotionState enum values for CaptureWaitHi, CaptureDamageHi, CapturePulledLw, and CaptureWaitLw with numeric comments 0x0E0 through 0x0E3.
Renamed one local in ftCo_800D8C54 from temp_r31 to fp and normalized Fighter_ChangeMotionState flags from 0U to 0.
smart_moves: Used small static inline wrappers for ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Phys_inline and fn_800DC624_inline to express shared logic while still satisfying matching constraints.
Used #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around selected functions where inlining behavior mattered for matching.
Kept state-change helper logic close to the callback implementations, making the high/low CaptureDamage transition structure easy to compare against assembly.
Added only the motion-variable fields needed for capturedamage, with explicit offsets and padding, rather than guessing a larger struct layout.
Handled model-space/world-space offsets through lb_8000B1CC and FtPart_XRotN instead of hardcoding position deltas, matching the apparent bone/JObj-based capture behavior.
Preserved animation command progress during cross-state transitions by using Ft_MF_UpdateCmd with fp->cur_anim_frame where the diff shows same-animation transitions.
Updated headers alongside C implementations so newly matched callbacks can be reused by other files without implicit or local-only declarations.
decomp_lessons: Capture/capturedamage callbacks often require both fighter state transitions and explicit position/JObj synchronization; matching one without the other may leave callback behavior incomplete.
When motion variables reveal a new union member, add the minimal known layout with offset comments and padding so future work can extend it safely.
For fighter callbacks that transition between related motion states, check whether the original code preserves current animation frame and update-command state rather than restarting the animation.
Yoshi capture interactions can need character-specific includes and special hurtbox/intangibility setup; do not assume common capture behavior is fully character-neutral.
Boolean guard fields such as x2226_b2 can determine whether ground/air collision transition callbacks are installed; preserve those guard conditions exactly.
Matching may require deliberate no-inline pragmas and inline helper extraction to reproduce compiler structure.
Header prototypes should include exact GObj type aliases used by the implementation, such as Fighter_GObj versus HSD_GObj, because callback signatures differ.
naming_conventions: Known fighter pointers are preferably named fp when evidence is clear, replacing generic compiler-register names like temp_r31 in touched code.
Unknown matched functions retain address-style names such as fn_800DAA40, fn_800DAD18, fn_800DC384, fn_800DC404, and fn_800DC624.
Motion-state enum names use ftCo_MS_CaptureDamageHi and ftCo_MS_CaptureDamageLw for high/low capture damage states.
Motion-variable union member added as capturedamage, with currently unknown fields left as x0, x4, pad_x8, and x18.
Callback function names follow the existing ftCo_<State>_<CallbackKind> pattern, such as ftCo_CaptureDamageHi_Phys and ftCo_CaptureDamageLw_Coll.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma dont_inline around ftCo_800DC284, ftCo_CaptureDamageHi_Phys, ftCo_800DC3A4, and fn_800DC404 to control code generation.
Used PAD_STACK in fn_800DAD18 and fn_800DC624 to reproduce required stack layout.
Split low CaptureDamage physical and collision-transition logic into static inline helpers to match call/inlining structure.
Preserved explicit Vec3 temporaries and field-by-field position additions rather than simplifying to helper operations.
Used direct gobj->user_data in some functions and GET_FIGHTER in others, reflecting codegen-sensitive access patterns from the match.
Kept HSD_JObjSetTranslate calls after state/position updates in low-to-high transition paths to synchronize the rendered JObj with fp->cur_pos.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but the provided human_text_excerpt was empty, so no specific reviewer or author discussion can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename fn_800DAA40, fn_800DAD18, fn_800DB5D8, fn_800DC384, fn_800DC404, or fn_800DC624.
Check whether ftCommon_MotionVars.capturedamage fields x0, x4, and x18 receive semantic names in subsequent work.
Search for other Yoshi capture or egg-related PRs that use ftYs_Init_8012BAC0 or similar hurtbox scaling.
Compare CapturePulledHi/Lw and CaptureDamageHi/Lw callbacks for repeated transition patterns that can guide future matching.
Look for additional uses of #pragma dont_inline and static inline wrappers in ftCommon matching PRs.
